name: Moat
description: Defensive water trench encircling a building's outer footprint. The AABB defines the OUTER footprint of the moat; the trench itself is a 2-3 block wide ring hugging the outer edge. A 1-block course of @secondary forms the impermeable trench floor at y0; a 1-block-tall @secondary outer retaining wall and an equivalent inner lip contain the water on both sides; minecraft:water fills the ring at y0+1 exactly 1 block deep; 2-3 minecraft:lily_pad float on the surface and 4 minecraft:cobblestone_wall corner posts crown the outer corners as decorative bollards. Sized 6×2×6..24×3×24; below 6×6 the ring would collapse into a solid 2×2 square with no interior.

required_furniture.rule: Ring of @secondary (stone) at y0, 2-3 blocks wide, covering the outer edge of the AABB footprint.
Fill minecraft:water along the trench between outer wall and inner lip from y0+1 to y0+1 (single layer, 1 block deep).
1-block-tall @secondary borders along BOTH the outer perimeter and the inner edge of the ring at y0+1; stone retains water on both sides.
minecraft:lily_pad floating at y0+2 over water cells (middle of each outer face); never on the lips.
Optional minecraft:cobblestone_wall on the four outer corners at y0+2, sitting atop the outer retaining wall.

placement_rules: Stone retains water on both sides
Outer retaining wall flush with the AABB outer face
Inner lip separates moat from interior
Lily pads only on water cells (never on lips)
Corner posts at the four outer corners (decorative bollards)
Minimum 6×6 footprint to leave an actual ring (smaller collapses)
lighting_ventilation: Open-air structure; no enclosed space to ventilate. Reflected daylight off the water surface lights the immediate ground; in fantasy variants the lily-pad scatter and sea-lantern corner posts add ambient glow at night.

medieval.notes: Classic cobblestone-revetted ditch with plain water. Cobblestone wall corner posts complete the castle-foot reading.

modern.notes: Architectural reflecting-pool reading: clean smooth_stone revetment, clear water, minimal corner posts.

furniture_overrides.rule: Bias toward 3 lily pads instead of 2; mossy water reads as enchanted.

fantasy.notes: Mossy cobblestone revetment with lily-pad-dense water; pairs with mossy curtain walls and dark-oak keeps.

style_affinity: Medieval / fortified-military motif. Coherent in medieval/castle/rustic/fantasy/gothic. Out of place in Modern, Minimalist, East-Asian, Classical/Renaissance buildings.
